# NextEngine

> Slogan: NextEngine, the future of creation.

NextEngine is a modular, high-performance, retro-futuristic game engine designed natively for autonomous AI agents (LLMs) and human developers. It is 100% Free Software released under the GNU General Public License v3 (GPL v3).

## Core Architecture

NextEngine combines C++ speed with Vulkan API visual pipelines, offering a lightweight alternative to bloated commercial engines.

### 1. LLM-Native Core (Agent Integration)
NextEngine is built from the ground up for LLMs. It bypasses complex binary scene editors, instead exposing human-readable command protocols:
- **JSON API State Descriptors**: LLMs can inspect, load, and modify engine scene structures via standardized JSON endpoints.
- **TCP Cognitive Socket Interface**: Run agent runtimes locally or on remote servers connecting via a simple TCP loop (`localhost:8090`).
- **Autonomous Compilation**: Agents can trigger asset generation, Lua/C++ script updates, and shader compilations dynamically at runtime.

### 2. Human Customizability & Control
Designed to give human engineers total programming control without slowing layers of abstraction:
- **Pure C++17/20 Execution**: Develop core scripts in standard high-performance C++.
- **ImGui Visual Panels**: Build debug overlays, custom menus, and hub utilities using lightweight Dear ImGui widgets.
- **NextEngine Hub**: Launcher utility (`NextEngineHub.exe`) designed for project organization, creation, templates, and launching.

### 3. Developer Manual & Tutorials (Chapters 01-15)
The engine website hosts a complete C++ game development manual detailing:
- **Core Engine APIs**: `ne::Node` structure, `ne::Scene` root, `ne::Behaviour` lifecycle scripting, `ne::ResourceManager` loading, `ne::Input` queries, and `ne::Camera` controls.
- **Visuals & Illumination**: `ne::LightNode` Point/Directional setups and shadow mapping switches.
- **Custom Editor Inspections**: Implementing custom GUI windows by overriding `onDrawInspector()` with Dear ImGui.
- **Standalone Packaging**: Building binaries with CMake compile commands.

### 4. Open Source & License
- **GPL v3 Free Software**: Independent core development, zero telemetry, zero cloud locks, and zero licensing fees. Modifying the engine core requires releasing modifications under GPL v3. Writing custom game scripts/assets using the engine APIs without modifying the core allows releasing and selling games under proprietary, closed-source licenses.
- **Repository**: [saias-o/NextEngine](https://github.com/saias-o/NextEngine)

---

## Technical Specifications

- **Graphics Backend**: Vulkan 1.2+
- **Minimum OS Compatibility**: Windows 10/11 x64, Linux Ubuntu 22.04+
- **Build System**: CMake 3.20+
- **Compilers**: MSVC 2019+, GCC 10+, Clang 11+

---

- **GitHub Repository**: [saias-o/NextEngine](https://github.com/saias-o/NextEngine)
- **Sponsorship Portal**: [GitHub Sponsors](https://github.com/sponsors/saias-o)

- **Linux Binaries**: [nextengine-0.1.0-linux.tar.gz](http://127.0.0.1:8082/nextengine-0.1.0-linux.tar.gz)
